note: SNG-092 — Erik-directed 2026-07-12. The old vocabulary had TEN verbs and `reveal` was 26% of every function-tag in the game — because `reveal` was doing the work of sense, track, appraise, read-a-mind AND foresee all at once. Twenty-four verbs in eight families. Compared against D&D 5e's schools, Blades in the Dark's actions, Fate and PbtA; the gaps below are the ones those systems all have and we did not.
theEmbarrassment: ⛔ We had a **Wrights [creation]** tradition and an **Unmakers [destruction]** tradition facing each other across an axis — **and no function tag for MAKING ANYTHING.** The creation people could not create. That is what a missing verb costs.
noDamageTypes: Erik ruled: **no separate damage-type system.** We do not need one — **the AXIS already is the damage type and SPECTRAL FIT already is the resistance.** A Blazeborn's harm is light-flavoured because its `axes` say so; an Umbral standing in it is already penalised by spectral misfit. **The system exists. It must simply reach the NARRATION and the receipt** — *'the light finds you where you are thinnest'* — rather than being a silent number.

HARM.notTheSameAs: `break` (that is for things). `hinder` (that weakens without wounding).
HARM.example: The Edge. Hunter's Strike. Kindle's flare.

HARM.definition: Harm or destroy a THING — an object, a structure, a working, a formation.
WEAKEN, drain, impair, or slow — without wounding. The debuff space.
HARM.example: The Grey Hand. Wither. The Dread.

RESTORE.definition: Undo a CONDITION — cleanse, cure, un-curse, un-rewrite. Not a wound; a STATE.
RESTORE.notTheSameAs: `heal` (a wound). ⛔ **Halvex Coil's rewritten victims have no cure because this verb did not exist.**
RESTORE.example: The Mercy. (Owed: a Rootkin and a Verist answer.)

RESTORE.definition: Make an ally BETTER — stronger, faster, braver, clearer. The buff space.
RESTORE.example: Steady Soul. Voice of the Flock. ⛔ **This space was EMPTY.**

PROTECT.notTheSameAs: `ward` (persistent, over a place or person). `resist` (an effect, not a blow).

PROTECT.definition: PERSISTENT protection over a place, a thing, or a person. It stands when you are not looking.
PROTECT.example: Prism Ward. Boundary-Stone. The Shielding Word.

PROTECT.definition: Withstand an EFFECT — fear, a compulsion, a working, a rewriting.
PROTECT.example: Stillcraft. The Held Breath. Total Focus.

KNOW.definition: Perceive what IS. The present, here.
KNOW.notTheSameAs: `foresee` (what WILL be). `track` (where a thing HAS BEEN).

KNOW.definition: Perceive what WILL be, or what is COMING.
KNOW.notTheSameAs: `reveal` (the present). ⛔ **The Hourkeepers can PRE-REMEMBER and there was no verb for it.**
KNOW.example: Hour-Sense. The Pre-Remembered. Chaos-Sense (the break before it breaks).

KNOW.definition: Follow a TRAIL — where a thing has been, where it is going.
KNOW.example: Wayfinding. Old Roads. The Long Grain.

SHAPE.definition: CREATE something that was not there.
SHAPE.example: ⛔ **THE MISSING VERB.** Makecraft. The Raised Thing. Tinker's Hand.

SHAPE.definition: Change a thing's NATURE into another.
CALL something to you, or open a way for it to come.

INFLUENCE.example: Command Engine. Beastfriend. ⛔ **`bind` was doing this job badly.**

INFLUENCE.definition: Show what is FALSE. Make them believe a thing that is not.
INFLUENCE.notTheSameAs: `conceal` (which hides what IS true). ⛔ **The Veilwrights' entire craft had no verb.**
INFLUENCE.example: Falsecraft. False Trail. The Kind Lie.
Shroud. Dim. Blend In. Quiet Step.

MOVE.definition: TACTICAL positioning — a step, a dodge, a leap, a shadow-step across a room.

MOVE.notTheSameAs: `move` (tactical). ⛔ **The Horizon-Walkers SPAN and it was tagged the same as a dodge.**
MOVE.example: Spanwork. The Long Stride. Shortfold. Waygate.

MOVE.definition: Make a WAY THROUGH — a lock, a door, a seal, a ward. **Without destroying it.**
MOVE.notTheSameAs: `break` (which destroys). ⛔ **The Umbrals OPEN what they do not break, and there was no verb.**
MOVE.example: Unmake Seal. Hold the Aperture. The Old Procedure.

SUSTAIN.definition: Food, water, warmth, shelter, endurance. **Staying alive when nothing is attacking you.**
SUSTAIN.notTheSameAs: `heal` (which mends a wound). ⛔ **NOBODY IN THIS WORLD COULD FEED THEMSELVES WITH A CRAFT.**

comparedTo.dnd5e: Abjuration→ward/shield/resist · Conjuration→summon/make/travel · Divination→reveal/foresee/track · Enchantment→command/bind · Evocation→strike/break/heal · Illusion→deceive/conceal · Necromancy→(our AXIS, not a school) · Transmutation→transform
comparedTo.bladesInTheDark: Hunt→track · Study→reveal · Survey→reveal · Tinker→mend/make · Finesse→move · Prowl→conceal · Skirmish→strike · Wreck→break · Attune→foresee/summon · Command→command · Consort/Sway→(social, our attribute layer)
comparedTo.whatTheyAllHaveThatWeDidNot: **make · summon · transform · command · foresee · deceive · empower · hinder · resist · sustain.** Every system in the field has these. We had none of them.

theRevealGlut: `reveal` was 26% of all function-tags — 62 of 142 abilities. **That is not because the world is observant. It is because `reveal` was the only verb available for sensing, tracking, appraising, mind-reading and foreseeing.** Splitting the verb self-corrects the count without changing a single ability's flavour.
